message.content:
import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'
import { YouTubeEmbed } from '@/components/mdx/YouTubeEmbed'

export const metadata = {
  title: "Bizarre Lineage Stats: Complete Guide & Best Builds 2026",
  description: "Master Bizarre Lineage stats with our comprehensive 2026 guide. Learn the best builds for White Snake, Anubis, and more to dominate Morioh Cho.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/1e293b/fff?text=Bizarre+Lineage+Stats+Guide",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Bizarre Lineage Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- **Bizarre lineage stats**: Understanding the difference between Strength and Destructive Power is crucial for maximizing damage.
- **Top Tier Builds**: White Snake and Made in Heaven require high Destructive Power, while C-Moon scales with Destructive Energy.
- **Stat Caps**: Most stats cap at 100, though some specific builds can push boundaries through Prestige bonuses.
- **Health Priority**: Always invest 25-30 points into Health early on to survive boss raids and NPC ganks.
- **Weapon Synergy**: Only invest in the Weapon stat if you are using Anubis or specialized kendo fighting styles.
</Callout>

Entering the world of Morioh Cho in this Roblox RPG requires more than just a powerful Stand; it demands a deep understanding of how to allocate your points. Whether you are a newcomer or a seasoned fighter aiming for Prestige 3, mastering your **bizarre lineage stats** is the fundamental difference between being a top-tier contender and getting wiped by basic NPCs. The game does not explicitly explain every mechanic, often leaving players to waste valuable skill point resets on sub-optimal configurations.

In this guide, we will break down every individual stat, from physical Strength to the complex Destructive Energy requirements of mythical Stands. We will also provide optimized builds for the current 2026 meta, ensuring your character is perfectly tuned for both PvE raids and high-stakes PvP encounters. By following these professional recommendations, you can ensure every Stat Point (SP) contributes to your ultimate goal of becoming the strongest Stand user in the lineage.

<Section icon={Map} title="Understanding Core Bizarre Lineage Stats">

Before diving into specific builds, you must understand what each attribute actually influences. A common mistake is confusing Strength with Destructive Power, which can lead to a build that does zero damage with a Stand.

| Stat | Primary Effect | Best For |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Strength** | Increases physical M1 (left-click) base attacks | Standless builds, Boxing, Karate |
| **Health** | Increases maximum HP capacity | All builds (Survival) |
| **Power** | Increases Sub-Ability damage and Stamina | Hamon, Vampire, Cyborg users |
| **Weapon** | Increases Slash and Weapon-specific damage | Anubis, Kendo, Odachi users |
| **Destructive Power** | Increases Stand M1s and physical Stand moves | Star Platinum, The World, King Crimson |
| **Destructive Energy** | Increases Stand Special/AoE Ability damage | C-Moon, Weather Report, White Snake |

**Video Highlights:**
- **Stat Definitions**: Learn the hidden difference between player M1s and Stand M1s.
- **Meta Builds**: A breakdown of the current S-tier Stand stat distributions.
- **Resetting Tips**: How to avoid wasting points during the leveling process.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="cyELdARlR1U" title="Bizarre Lineage Best Stats Guide" />

<Callout type="warning" title="Critical Distinction">
Strength **does not** increase your Stand's damage. If you are using a Stand-heavy playstyle, leave Strength at 0 and focus entirely on Destructive Power. Strength is only for players using fighting styles without their Stand summoned.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="Best Stand Builds for the 2026 Meta">

The optimal allocation of **bizarre lineage stats** depends entirely on which Stand you have equipped. Some Stands rely on raw physical barrage damage, while others depend on massive energy-consuming ultimates.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="White Snake">
    - **Destructive Power**: 100
    - **Destructive Energy**: 25
    - **Health**: 25-30
    - Focuses on high move damage and utility.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Anubis">
    - **Destructive Power**: 80-90
    - **Weapon**: 40
    - **Health**: 30
    - Hybrid build utilizing both Stand moves and sword M1s.
  </Card>
  <Card title="C-Moon">
    - **Destructive Energy**: 100
    - **Health**: 50
    - **Destructive Power**: 0
    - Relies on massive AoE ability damage.
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

For more specialized Stands, refer to the following data table to ensure you aren't misallocating points into stats that don't scale with your abilities.

| Stand | Health | Destructive Power | Destructive Energy | Other |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **The World** | 25 | 105 | 20 | Focus on raw DP |
| **Made in Heaven** | 30 | 90 | 35 | High energy cost |
| **King Crimson** | 25 | 120 | 5 | Maximize burst DP |
| **TW High Voltage** | 25 | 55 | 70 | Hybrid Energy build |
| **Star Platinum** | 20 | 100 | 30 | Classic physical build |

<Callout type="tip" title="Pro Strategy">
If you are playing as King Crimson, pushing Destructive Power to 120 (via Prestige bonuses) allows you to "one-shot" many common NPCs and significantly speeds up your grinding efficiency.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Fighting Styles and Sub-Abilities">

Your **bizarre lineage stats** are also influenced by your choice of Fighting Style and Sub-Ability. These provide passive bonuses and active moves that can complement your Stand's weaknesses.

| Fighting Style | Key Moves | Obtainment Location |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Boxing** | White Fang, Dempsey Roll | Gym Building (Bus Stop 2) |
| **Karate** | Sixth Massacre, Seiken | Near Hospital (Bus Stop 15) |
| **Kendo** | Silent Edge, Rising Tempest | Kame Yu Market (Bus Stop 15) |

Sub-Abilities offer even more utility. For example, the Vampire sub-ability is currently ranked as S-Tier due to its passive health regeneration, which offsets a lower Health stat.

| Sub-Ability | Passive Bonus | Scaling Stat |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Vampire** | HP Regeneration | Power / Destructive Energy |
| **Cyborg** | +15% Defense | Power |
| **Hamon** | HP Regen / Burn Damage | Power |

<Checklist
  id="sub-ability-goals"
  title="Sub-Ability Checklist:"
  items={[
    "Obtain Vampire Mask or visit NPC for Hamon/Cyborg",
    "Reach Level 25 to unlock first Sub-Ability slot",
    "Invest at least 20 points into 'Power' for move scaling",
    "Sync Sub-Ability moves with Stand stun-locks"
  ]}
/>

<Callout type="success" title="Defense Meta">
The Cyborg sub-ability's 15% defense buff is essential for players who prefer a "tank" build. Pair this with C-Moon's high health for a nearly unkillable character in 2026 PvP.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Trophy} title="How to Awaken and Prestige">

To reach the maximum potential of your **bizarre lineage stats**, you must undergo Awakening and Prestige. These processes unlock higher level caps and exclusive stat-boosting items.

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="Reach Max Level">
    Grind to Level 50 and reach Stand Conjuration Level 100.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="The Gym Trial">
    Visit the Gym (Bus Stop 2), sit on a mat, and speak to the NPC to start "Surpass My Limits."
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="Raid Bosses">
    Defeat Jotaro Kujo, Muhammad Avdol, and DIO in Raids.
  </Step>
  <Step num="4" title="World Events">
    Participate in 50 World Events to complete Stand Awakening 2.
  </Step>
  <Step num="5" title="Prestige">
    Visit the Arch Mage at TC Hospital (Bus Stop 10) with 10,000 Cash to reset and gain Prestige Points.
  </Step>
</Steps>

| Prestige Level | Reward | Requirement |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Prestige 1** | 5 Prestige Points + 1 Storage Slot | Level 50 + 10k Cash |
| **Prestige 2** | 5 Prestige Points + 1 Storage Slot | Level 50 + 10k Cash |
| **Prestige 3** | 5 Prestige Points + 1 Storage Slot | Level 50 + 10k Cash |

<Callout type="info" title="Prestige Tip">
Use your Prestige Points at the Arch Mage to buy **Stand Stat Essences**. These are the only way to push certain stats past the natural 100 cap for "God-tier" builds.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Bizarre Lineage FAQ">

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "What are the most important bizarre lineage stats for beginners?",
    answer: "Beginners should prioritize Health (up to 25) and Destructive Power. This ensures you can survive NPC encounters while dealing enough damage to farm effectively."
  },
  {
    question: "Does Strength affect my Stand's barrage damage?",
    answer: "No. Strength only affects your character's physical M1s. Stand barrages and physical moves scale exclusively with Destructive Power."
  },
  {
    question: "How do I reset my stats if I made a mistake?",
    answer: "You can use Skill Point Resets, which are often available via codes or can be purchased in the in-game shop. Alternatively, reaching a new Prestige level will reset your points."
  },
  {
    question: "Why is Destructive Energy important for C-Moon?",
    answer: "C-Moon's most powerful moves are considered 'Special Abilities' rather than physical attacks. High Destructive Energy maximizes the damage of its gravity-shifting skills."
  }
]} />

</Section>
